methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Defense & Security (found 1 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 19 same-sector peers exist in Defense & Security, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Evolv Technology
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Public), post-money $1.04B

data.narrative:
Kraken Robotics is a public-stage company in the Defense & Security sector. The comparable universe consists of 2 public peers (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private peers, including Evolv Technology, Anduril Industries, Helsing, Shield AI, and Saronic. The sector encompasses 19 same-sector peers total.

The peer-median method is not applicable to this valuation. The Defense & Security sector lacks sufficient narrow peer clustering: only 1 same-stage comparable exists, with 0 adjacent-stage and 0 similar-tier peers meeting screening criteria. This represents an insufficiency relative to the minimum threshold of 3 datapoints required for meaningful median-based triangulation. Expanding to all 19 sector peers would introduce material heterogeneity in stage, business model, and technology maturity, rendering such a median unreliable.

Without triangulation via peer-median, this analysis cannot support a specific valuation range based on the provided data. Alternative methods—such as discounted cash flow, transaction comps, or revenue multiples applied to the identified public and private peer set—would require financial metrics (revenue, margins, growth rates, or recent transaction values) not present in the dataset. A complete valuation would require either narrower peer identification within Defense & Security or disclosure of Kraken's financial performance and comparable transaction data.
